questions.q: Explain the concept of sustainable development.
questions.answer: Sustainable development is a pattern of economic growth and resource use that aims to meet the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs. It involves a balance between economic development, social equity, and environmental protection.

questions.q: What are the consequences of environmental degradation?
questions.answer: Environmental degradation can lead to loss of biodiversity, climate change, water pollution, soil erosion, and air pollution, ultimately affecting human health and economic stability.

questions.q: Describe the concept of carrying capacity.
questions.answer: Carrying capacity is the maximum number of people or level of consumption that an environment can support without suffering significant harm. It is the ability of a system to support a particular level of human activity without degrading the environment.

questions.q: Explain the significance of the Brundtland Commission.
questions.answer: The Brundtland Commission was established in 1983 to study the concept of sustainable development. It defined sustainable development as meeting the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs.

questions.q: What are the main features of the Rio Declaration?
questions.answer: The Rio Declaration is a set of principles adopted at the 1992 Earth Summit in Rio de Janeiro. Its main features include the precautionary principle, the polluter pays principle, and the principle of common but differentiated responsibilities.

Biodiversity refers to the variety of different species of plants, animals, and microorganisms that live in an ecosystem or on Earth as a whole. It includes genetic diversity, species diversity, and ecosystem diversity.

questions.q: Explain the concept of natural resources.
questions.answer: Natural resources are materials and substances that occur naturally in the environment, such as water, air, soil, forests, minerals, and fossil fuels.

questions.q: Describe the impact of human activities on the environment.
questions.answer: Human activities such as deforestation, pollution, climate change, and overconsumption of resources have significant impacts on the environment, leading to loss of biodiversity, climate change, and environmental degradation.
Conservation refers to the protection and preservation of natural resources, ecosystems, and species to maintain their health and integrity for future generations.

questions.q: Describe the concept of sustainable consumption.
questions.answer: Sustainable consumption refers to the use of resources in a way that meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs. It involves reducing consumption, reusing resources, and recycling waste.
